opp.name: Chemours [Intake/SRM/CLM]
opp.next_step: Hope you are having a great week. I’m following up on our previous discussion regarding Chemours' procurement environment and your ongoing landscape transition. We previously touched on the complexities of managing SAP Ariba, Sirion, and JAGGAER alongside your S/4 HANA journey, and how a unified suite could help streamline contract visibility and supplier risk. I wanted to check in and see how your project status and timeline are progressing, and if any new priorities have surfaced. As your team continues to navigate these systems, we would be glad to share a quick, tailored look at how our AI tools, like Merlin Intake, integrate smoothly with core ERP environments to simplify user adoption. If you or your team are open to a brief sync or a deep-dive on a specific module in the coming weeks, please let me know what your schedule looks like.

Maybe we form a formal project in 2026, we'll see (Timothy Duprey, 12 Mar 2026)
Timothy Duprey stated 'I'm not the project driver' and has not re-engaged since 12 Mar despite rep outreach 24 Jun

sources: Avoma discovery call 12 Mar 2026, Timothy Duprey
SF Next_Step__c 24 Jun 2026 (rep-side outreach, no buyer quantification)
metrics.narrative: Timothy Duprey articulated operational pain around managing three disconnected systems (SAP Ariba for sourcing, Sirion for CLM, JAGGAER for tail spend) alongside an ongoing S/4HANA migration, noting that 'visibility across contracts and supplier risk is fragmented' and 'our users are jumping between platforms' (discovery call 12 Mar 2026). No quantified financial impact, headcount burden, cycle-time cost, or ROI target has been stated. The business problem is confirmed qualitatively but the value case remains unquantified, which is typical for a pre-project exploratory conversation but fatal for a deal 185 days from close in Qualified stage. Next Step outreach from 24 Jun references 'streamline contract visibility and supplier risk' but records no buyer reply confirming urgency or scale. The gap is not that pain is absent but that it has never been tied to a budget-justifying number, and no economic buyer has been engaged to validate the business case.

sources: Avoma discovery call 12 Mar 2026, Timothy Duprey: 'I'm not the project driver'
Salesforce OpportunityContactRole: only Timothy listed, no Champion role
No subsequent buyer engagement after 12 Mar despite rep outreach 24 Jun
champion.narrative: No champion exists. Timothy Duprey is the sole engaged contact and explicitly stated 'I'm not the project driver' (discovery call 12 Mar 2026), positioning himself as an interested observer within the CET group rather than an advocate with decision influence. He has provided no introductions, no shared internal documents, no competitive intelligence, no agenda-setting, and no evidence of advocacy. He has not re-engaged since the 12 Mar call despite a rep follow-up on 24 Jun (13 days ago). A champion requires access to the economic buyer or decision maker, willingness to advocate internally, and active engagement; Timothy meets none of these criteria. For a deal 185 days from close in Qualified stage, the absence of a champion is disqualifying. The activation is to ask Timothy directly for an introduction to at least one CET decision-maker and to assess whether he is willing to sponsor that introduction; if not, this deal should be re-staged to Discovery or Omitted.

sources: Avoma discovery call 12 Mar 2026, Timothy Duprey: SAP Ariba, Sirion, JAGGAER incumbents; Ariba modernization stalled
SF Competitors__c: blank (hygiene gap)
Known insight topics: SAP Ariba, Jaggaer, Sirion
competition.narrative: Chemours currently operates SAP Ariba (sourcing), Sirion (CLM), and JAGGAER (tail spend) as incumbent platforms, with SAP Ariba being the primary system (discovery call 12 Mar 2026, Timothy Duprey). Timothy noted that a prior 'Ariba modernization effort stalled when the S/4HANA migration paused,' indicating that SAP Ariba modernization (potentially to Ariba 2.0 or SAP S/4HANA-native procurement) is a live alternative path and likely the default if Chemours' ERP team regains momentum. No other vendor was mentioned by name in the call, but the presence of Sirion and JAGGAER as embedded solutions means Zycus must displace three systems to win, not just one. The wedge is platform consolidation (one suite vs three tools) plus AI-driven intake automation (Merlin) that none of the incumbents deliver natively in a unified UX, plus low-friction ERP integration that avoids the complexity Timothy cited as a user-adoption killer. However, the stalled Ariba modernization is both an opening (frustration with SAP's execution) and a risk (if SAP restarts and offers an all-SAP stack story, Chemours may default to the familiar path). How we win: position Zycus as the unified, AI-native alternative that executes now while SAP's roadmap remains uncertain, and make Merlin Intake the centerpiece of a Phase 1 quick-win tied to their S/4HANA go-live.

sources: Avoma discovery call 12 Mar 2026, Timothy Duprey
identify_pain.narrative: Timothy Duprey confirmed that Chemours is managing SAP Ariba (sourcing), Sirion (CLM), and JAGGAER (tail spend) as three disconnected platforms alongside an S/4HANA migration, and that 'visibility across contracts and supplier risk is fragmented' with 'users jumping between platforms' (discovery call 12 Mar 2026). He is the operational pain owner for the procurement technology landscape within the CET group context. The pain is real, confirmed, and directly stated. The gap is not pain identification but pain quantification (no cost/cycle-time/error-rate numbers) and pain escalation (no executive-level acknowledgment or budget allocation). The pain is sufficient to justify continued discovery but insufficient to justify a Qualified forecast without a sponsor willing to fund a solution.

sources: Avoma discovery call 12 Mar 2026 (no contracting discussion)
SF Current_Contract_Expiration__c: blank
No legal/procurement synthesis fields populated
paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, procurement, or InfoSec process has been discussed. No MSA, SOW, or redline workflow. No SI or partner involvement mentioned. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c forcing function. The 12 Mar discovery call was product-focused (platform needs, AI interest) with no commercial or contracting thread. For a $100k SaaS deal in a Fortune 500 chemical manufacturer, InfoSec review, data residency, ERP integration approvals, and legal terms are certain gates, but none have been surfaced yet. This gap is expected at true discovery but fatal for a deal staged as Qualified with a Jan 2027 close. The activation is to ask Timothy (or a CET contact once identified) what Chemours' standard procurement and legal workflow looks like for a platform purchase of this size and complexity.

sources: Avoma discovery call 12 Mar 2026, Timothy Duprey: 'governed by an internal CET group', 'I'm not the project driver'
Salesforce OpportunityContactRole query (only Timothy Duprey present, no EB/Sponsor role)
SF X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c: blank
economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified. Salesforce contact roles contain only Timothy Duprey (title unknown, role Influencer inferred). No Executive Sponsor, no CFO-level or procurement-head contact. The synthesis fields (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c) are unpopulated. Timothy stated on the 12 Mar call that 'the project is governed by an internal CET group' but provided no names, and explicitly said 'I'm not the project driver.' No call transcript shows a commercial or pricing conversation, and no Salesforce task or event names a budget approver. The sole evidence of budget authority is the qualitative reference to a CET governance group with no identified leader or sponsor. For a $100k deal 185 days from close this is a disqualifying gap. The activation move is to demand access to at least one CET member with budget authority and decision rights within the next two weeks.

sources: Avoma discovery call 12 Mar 2026, Timothy Duprey: 'governed by an internal CET group', 'maybe 2026'
SF What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c: blank
Call sequence: only one buyer call, one participant, no escalation
decision_process.narrative: The only process intelligence is Timothy's statement that 'the project is governed by an internal CET group' with a speculative timeline of 'maybe we form a formal project in 2026, we'll see' (discovery call 12 Mar 2026). No approval chain, no procurement workflow, no stakeholder sequence, and no formal evaluation structure has been described. The call sequence itself shows only one buyer participant (Timothy) across one call, with no escalation, no executive join, no multi-department representation. Salesforce fields (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Multiple_approvals__c) are all blank. There is no evidence of a defined process; this deal is pre-process. For a Qualified stage opportunity 185 days from close, the absence of a known decision process is disqualifying. The activation is to secure a second meeting with at least one CET member and map the governance, approval, and timeline explicitly.

sources: Avoma discovery call 12 Mar 2026, Timothy Duprey
SF Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c: blank
decision_criteria.narrative: Timothy described Chemours' desire for 'a unified suite that handles intake, sourcing, contracts, and invoicing in one place' and noted that 'AI-driven intake to reduce manual ticket triage' was of interest, particularly given their S/4HANA context (discovery call 12 Mar 2026). He also mentioned that any new platform would need to 'play nice with SAP' and that 'user adoption is always the killer; our people hate switching tools.' These are directional buying criteria (platform consolidation, ERP integration, AI automation, low adoption friction) but they are not yet formalized, prioritized, or weighted. No RFP, no BRD, no scoring rubric has been shared. The criteria are inferred from a single exploratory conversation rather than confirmed through a structured evaluation process. For a deal in Qualified stage this is a significant gap; decision criteria should be documented and agreed with the buying committee by now. The synthesis fields (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c) are blank, and no subsequent call has refined or validated the 12 Mar conversation.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — Merlin Intake (AI-driven intake automation) was mentioned positively by Timothy Duprey as 'of interest' to reduce manual ticket triage (12 Mar 2026). However, no demo, no use-case validation, no workshop, and no executive-level AI narrative has been delivered. The AI positioning is a stated interest with no proof of value or executive buy-in, and no Salesforce AIS score is recorded. Under-positioned: the agentic Merlin story has not been put in front of the buyer's decision-makers or tied to a business case.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 10 is carried by differentiation strength +0.30 (weight 20); competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 46 vs stage-expected 50.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 46 (roughly flat): no activity instrumentation on this record yet (cadence reads fully after its next sweep) — Salesforce shows activity 1d ago. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 1 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (10), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 46, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

champion_strength.source: Avoma discovery call 12 Mar 2026, Salesforce activity log
champion_strength.summary: No champion exists. Timothy Duprey is an interested influencer who explicitly disclaimed project ownership and has not engaged in 117 days. No other contact has been identified or engaged. For a deal 185 days from close, the absence of a champion with budget access and advocacy is disqualifying.

items.action: Demand access to at least one CET governance group member with budget authority and decision rights. Frame the ask as: 'Timothy, to tailor our approach to Chemours' S/4HANA and platform-consolidation priorities, I need 30 minutes with the CET lead or sponsor who will charter the project and own the business case. Can you introduce me this week?' If Timothy cannot or will not, re-stage this deal to Discovery or Omitted.
items.trigger: Timothy Duprey stated 'the project is governed by an internal CET group' but provided no names and said 'I'm not the project driver'
items.expected_effect: Identifies the economic buyer or project sponsor, validates whether a real buying process exists, and determines whether this deal is qualified or speculative. Without this access within 7 days, the deal should be removed from forecast.

items.action: If CET access is secured, run a 60-minute Merlin Intake AI demo focused on automated ticket triage and S/4HANA integration, with the CET sponsor and at least one procurement or IT stakeholder present. Position Merlin as the Phase 1 quick-win that delivers value while the broader platform decision is being shaped, and anchor the demo to a quantified business case (ticket volume, triage hours, cost per manual intake).
items.trigger: Timothy expressed interest in 'AI-driven intake to reduce manual ticket triage' but no demo, workshop, or use-case validation has occurred
items.expected_effect: Proves Merlin value, engages decision-makers, and creates a wedge against the stalled Ariba modernization path by demonstrating execution-ready AI automation.

items.action: Map the S/4HANA migration timeline and dependencies with the CET sponsor or the ERP program lead. Explicitly ask: 'How does your procurement platform decision interact with the S/4HANA go-live? Is the Ariba modernization still on hold, or is SAP back in the conversation?' Use the answer to position Zycus as either the execution-ready alternative (if SAP is stalled) or the best-of-breed complement (if SAP is restarting).
items.trigger: Prior Ariba modernization stalled when S/4HANA migration paused; dependency unresolved and represents both opportunity and risk
items.expected_effect: Clarifies the competitive landscape (SAP Ariba vs Zycus), identifies forcing functions or blockers tied to ERP timeline, and informs the wedge strategy.

items.action: Executive connect: have the deal owner's manager reach out to a Chemours procurement or IT executive (VP or above) with a peer-level message: 'We've been exploring how Zycus can help Chemours consolidate your Ariba/Sirion/JAGGAER landscape and accelerate AI-driven intake automation alongside your S/4HANA journey. I'd value 20 minutes to understand your priorities and share how we've helped similar chemical manufacturers execute faster than SAP's modernization roadmap allows.' Use this to leapfrog the single-threaded Timothy relationship and access the real decision layer.
items.trigger: 117 days since last buyer call, single-threaded contact who is not a driver, no economic buyer or sponsor identified
items.expected_effect: Breaks through the single-thread blocker, surfaces the real buying committee and decision process, and establishes executive sponsorship if a genuine project exists. If no response, confirms the deal is not real.

items.action: If none of the above moves generate buyer engagement or CET access within 30 days, recommend re-staging this opportunity to Discovery (if Timothy remains responsive and willing to facilitate introductions) or Omitted (if he does not). Update Amount to $0 or a discovery-stage placeholder, and push CloseDate to Q2 2027 or later to reflect the true, speculative timeline.
items.trigger: Deal staged as Qualified with $100k and Jan 2027 close, but evidence shows no project charter, no budget, no buying committee, and 117 days of buyer silence
items.expected_effect: Aligns forecast and stage with reality, removes a false-positive Pipeline deal from the forecast, and frees capacity to focus on qualified opportunities with real momentum.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 16 Sep 2025, 294 days old, 185 days to close (Jan 2027). Time in Qualified stage unknown due to missing submission dates. Last activity 13 days ago (24 Jun 2026). The close date implies a mid-cycle commercial stage, yet the deal has no identified economic buyer, no decision process, no champion, and the sole contact explicitly stated he is not driving the project. A qualified deal 185 days from close with no buyer-side project structure is indefensible. Required pace to reach Vendor Selected by Q4 2026 (to support Jan 2027 close) demands forming a buying committee, mapping the CET governance group, and securing executive sponsorship within 60 days. Current trajectory shows no such velocity.

best_practice_check.summary: This deal violates multiple enterprise-qualification best practices: single-threaded contact who is not the project driver, no economic buyer or champion identified, no formal decision process or timeline, and no forward movement in 117 days since the last buyer call. It is staged as Qualified with a Jan 2027 close but lacks the stakeholder, process, and commitment indicators required to support that forecast. The deal should be re-staged to Discovery or Omitted unless executive access is secured within 14 days.

items.quote: Any new platform would need to play nice with SAP
User adoption is always the killer; our people hate switching tools

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Chemours desires platform consolidation (one suite for intake, sourcing, contracts, invoicing), AI-driven intake automation, seamless S/4HANA integration, and low user-adoption friction. Zycus product scope (eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin Intake, ANA) is well-aligned on paper. Exposure is lack of proof: no demo, no workshop, no use-case validation, and no executive-level discussion to confirm fit beyond a single contact's directional interest.

hard.ais_why:
• Chemours category manager Timothy openly said he wants to understand how AI tools can help his role — a direct, customer-voice AI inquiry.
• Timothy framed AI as a way to catch up and work more efficiently, tying the interest to ongoing Ariba/S4HANA migration frustration.
• A formal project is only expected to form around 2026, so the AI conversation is real but the timing is forward; last activity 3/24/26.
hard.next_step:
Hope you are having a great week.

I’m following up on our previous discussion regarding Chemours' procurement environment and your ongoing landscape transition. We previously touched on the complexities of managing SAP Ariba, Sirion, and JAGGAER alongside your S/4 HANA journey, and how a unified suite could help streamline contract visibility and supplier risk.

I wanted to check in and see how your project status and timeline are progressing, and if any new priorities have surfaced.

As your team continues to navigate these systems, we would be glad to share a quick, tailored look at how our AI tools, like Merlin Intake, integrate smoothly with core ERP environments to simplify user adoption. If you or your team are open to a brief sync or a deep-dive on a specific module in the coming weeks, please let me know what your schedule looks like.

packets.subject: Timothy Duprey

value.name: SAP Ariba
value.quote: A prior Ariba modernization effort stalled when the S/4HANA migration paused
value.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the unified, AI-native platform that executes now while SAP's roadmap is uncertain; make Merlin Intake the Phase 1 quick-win tied to S/4HANA go-live, demonstrating faster time-to-value than restarting Ariba modernization.

value.name: SAP Ariba
value.quote: "Tail-spend leakage," poor catalog governance, and high off-contract purchases despite using Ariba P2P.
value.how_we_win: Position unified eProcurement + catalog governance to close the tail-spend and off-contract leakage Ariba has not solved.

value.name: SAP Ariba
value.quote: A lot of it was gonna depend on us migrating to S/4HANA... now we're back and looking at the management portal of using Ariba with mainly a transactional level.
value.how_we_win: Position the full source-to-pay suite (eProcurement/eInvoicing/Merlin Intake/ANA) as the modernization path that doesn't depend on the stalled S/4HANA migration.

packets.subject: SAP Ariba

value.name: JAGGAER
value.quote: Managing SAP Ariba, Sirion, and JAGGAER as three disconnected platforms
value.how_we_win: Tail-spend plus core-sourcing in one platform with AI intake, vs JAGGAER as a separate tail-only tool.

value.name: Jaggaer
value.quote: Lack of leveraging Ariba for RFX space (using Jaggaer instead) and challenges with supplier risk and performance management (KPIs, analytics).
value.how_we_win: Bring RFX/sourcing into the same platform as P2P to eliminate the separate Jaggaer workflow and its disconnected supplier-performance data.

value.name: Jaggaer
value.quote: We use Jagger for that, or RFIs and RFQs and data gathering.
value.how_we_win: Show Zycus eSourcing/RFX automation consolidating Jaggaer's function onto the same platform as procurement and contracts.

packets.subject: Jaggaer

value.name: Sirion
value.quote: Managing SAP Ariba, Sirion, and JAGGAER as three disconnected platforms
value.how_we_win: Consolidation story: replace Sirion CLM plus Ariba plus JAGGAER with one Zycus suite, eliminating integration tax and user friction.

value.name: Sirion
value.quote: Using a separate system (Sirion) for contract management, leading to separate databases and poor user experience/dashboards.
value.how_we_win: Consolidate contract lifecycle management into the same platform to remove the separate database and dashboard fragmentation Sirion causes.

packets.subject: Sirion
